metadata.seed: I just feel Helpless and hopeless, really. Like I said, just finding a job. I really don't know what I want to do anymore. After I graduated, I thought I was gonna get into business, but I just really don't know if that's for me. My parents always kind of gave me everything too. So the reason why I have my own apartment is because they bought it for me. But it was under the conditions of having to be in the same town as them. And so it's like I'm at a loss and I could continue just loving off my parents money, but then that kind of feels. I feel like I don't have a purpose. Like my purpose really just going to be to live off my parents money and kind of do whatever they want me to do and just to keep their money and their resources. So I'm just kind of at a loss right now in my life.

patient.query: I just feel Helpless and hopeless, really. Like I said, just finding a job. I really don't know what I want to do anymore. And so it's like I'm at a loss and I could continue just loving off my parents money, but then that kind of feels. I feel like I don't have a purpose. Like my purpose really just going to be to live off my parents money and kind of do whatever they want me to do and just to keep their money and their resources. So I'm just kind of at a loss right now in my life.

triggers: living in the same town as parents
automatic_thoughts: I don't know what I want to do
business may not be for me
I feel like I don't have a purpose
I'm just living off my parents' money

entailment: Loss of hope for the future (finding)

llm_response.response: Have you ever considered asking a friend or family member for their thoughts on your situation? It might help you see things from a different perspective.

validate_and_reflect.response: You seem to be feeling lost and unsure about your future, especially regarding finding a job and figuring out what you truly want. It's understandable to feel this way after graduation, and it's okay not to have everything figured out right away. Let's explore these feelings together and see if we can find some clarity.

patient.query: I guess I have thought about it, but I’m not sure. I mean, it’s tricky. I don’t want to burden anyone with my issues or feel like I’m relying too much on them. Sometimes, I just feel like it’s better to keep things to myself.

automatic_thoughts: I don't want to burden anyone with my issues
I might be relying too much on them
It's better to keep things to myself

entailment: Fear of being a burden to others (finding)
neutral: Fear of not coping with treatment (finding)

patient.query: Yeah, I get that. I just worry that if I open up, people might see me differently, or maybe think I’m weak. I mean, it feels like everyone else has it all figured out, and I’m just... struggling to keep up.

automatic_thoughts: people might see me differently
people might think I’m weak
everyone else has it all figured out
I’m just struggling to keep up

entailment: Fear of saying the wrong thing (finding)
Fear of being a burden to others (finding)
neutral: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

llm_response.response:
It's completely normal to feel this way sometimes. Vulnerability can be scary, but it's also a sign of strength. Remember, everyone has their own battles and struggles, even if they don't always show it on the surface. Sharing your feelings with trusted friends or a mental health professional can help lighten the load and bring you closer to others.

Also, try not to compare yourself to others. Comparison often leads to feelings of inadequacy. Instead, focus on your own progress and celebrate your small victories. Remember, it's okay to ask for help when you need it. You don't have to carry the weight of the world on your shoulders alone.

Lastly, remember that being open about your struggles can inspire others to do the same and create a supportive community. So, take one step at a time, and know that it's okay to ask for help when you need it. You are stronger than you think.

validate_and_reflect.response: I understand that you might be feeling a bit vulnerable about opening up to people. It's common to worry about how others perceive us, but remember, it takes courage to share our feelings. Let's work together on building your confidence and exploring ways to manage these concerns.

retrieved_concepts_used.concept: Fear of saying the wrong thing (finding)
retrieved_concepts_used.label: entailment

socratic_questioning.response: "It sounds like you're concerned about how others might perceive you if you open up. Have you ever considered that sharing your feelings could also bring you closer to people, rather than pushing them away?"

patient.query: I appreciate that, but it’s hard not to compare myself. I see people moving forward in their careers, and I just feel... stuck. Like, what am I doing wrong? Sometimes I think if I just pushed harder or was more assertive, things would change, but when I try, it just doesn't seem to work out. It’s frustrating.

triggers: seeing people moving forward in their careers
automatic_thoughts: I am doing something wrong
if I pushed harder, things would change
when I try, it doesn't work out

neutral: Personality trait typical of long term functioning (finding)

llm_response.response: I understand that you're feeling stuck and frustrated, especially when you see others progressing in their careers. It's natural to compare ourselves to others, but it can be challenging. Let's explore together what might be holding you back and how we can work on finding a solution that feels right for you.

What do you think they would tell you?

patient.query: That sounds good, but I don’t always know what’s really holding me back. Maybe it’s just this fear of failing or not being good enough. It’s like this voice in my head tells me I’ll never measure up. It makes it hard to even think about taking risks or trying something new.

automatic_thoughts: fear of failing
not being good enough
I'll never measure up

llm_response.response: What if we explored the possibility that you have more potential than this voice in your head suggests? Could there be other ways to look at this situation that might help you feel less held back?

validate_and_reflect.response: I understand that you're struggling with these thoughts about not measuring up and fear of failure. It can be overwhelming, but remember it's okay to have those feelings. Let's work together to explore where they come from and find ways to manage them.

socratic_questioning.response: What evidence do you have that this fear is stopping you from doing what you want? Let's explore it together.

alternative_perspective.response: What if we explored the possibility that you have more potential than this voice in your head suggests? Could there be other ways to look at this situation that might help you feel less held back?

patient.query: I guess that could be nice to think about, but it feels kind of scary, too. Like, what if I believe that and still don’t see any change? I mean, it’s hard to let go of that voice when it feels so... real, you know? It’s just there all the time, reminding me of my doubts.

automatic_thoughts: What if I believe that and still don’t see any change?
It's hard to let go of that voice when it feels so real.
It's just there all the time, reminding me of my doubts.

entailment: Ability to resist dangerous thoughts (observable entity)
neutral: Ability to resist obsessional thoughts (observable entity)
